code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test14-APR-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, suggesting possible glomerular damage and impaired tubular reabsorption. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. The findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possibly diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
